symbols: monastic cloak — renunciation of worldly power and spiritual warfare greater than earthly battle
gold halo — divine sanctification and glorification of the saint
angels at the top — heavenly endorsement and the communion between the earthly saint and the celestial realm
prayerful gesture — intercession and the saint's ongoing spiritual ministry before God's throne
dark background — the asceticism and hiddenness of the monastic life
description: A full-length figure of a bearded man stands frontally on a dark, narrow panel. He wears a long brown-gold monastic cloak over a dark garment, with a prominent golden halo around his head. His right hand is raised in a gesture of prayer or blessing, and his left hand holds an object near his waist. At the top of the panel, two small winged angels flank a vision or emblem. The icon is rendered in the traditional Byzantine style with a dark, weathered background and gold leaf accents.

scripture_refs.reason: The faith of those who conquered kingdoms and became mighty in war, applicable to the hagiographic tradition of warrior-saints like Alexander Nevsky.
The spiritual warfare that transcends earthly combat, reflected in Nevsky's choice to take monastic vows.
The great multitude of the redeemed standing before the throne, the heavenly reality to which the icon points.
